growth_records.medium: none (obligate intranuclear symbiont; no axenic growth medium, growth is intracellular within host amoeba nucleus)
growth_records.cite: Schulz et al. Life in an unusual intracellular niche: a bacterial symbiont infecting the nucleus of amoebae. The ISME Journal (2014). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2014.5. [PMC4817620]
growth_records.snippet: Following invasion of the nucleus, the symbionts start replicating, and after 24 h, two to four bacteria were present per nucleus. Up to 32 bacteria could be found 48 h p.i., suggesting a doubling time of about 8 h at th
growth_records.notes: Doubling time of ~8 h is explicitly stated by the authors, inferred from bacterial counts per host (Hartmannella sp. FS5) nucleus over time (2-4 bacteria/nucleus at 24 h p.i.; up to 32 bacteria/nucleus at 48 h p.i.). growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/8 = 0.0866 h-1 (calculated from the paper's stated doubling time). This is intracellular, non-axenic growth of an obligate symbiont; the paper explicitly states 'Growth of purified Nucleicultrix was also not observed under host-free conditions using a variety of standard laboratory media', so no medium composition or defined culture conditions exist. Included as the only quantitative growth observation in this paper; low confidence because it is an approximate count-based estimate for an intracellular bacterium, not a standard culture growth curve. [metadata completed from paper Methods: temperature]
